Docker `Error while fetching server API version` on Mac — Here's the Fix
I recently updated my Mac and Docker stopped working completely no matter what I tried. Eventually figured out the fix, so here it is.
I updated macOS to Big Sur 11.0.1 and Docker started throwing this whenever I tried to run anything:

docker.errors.DockerException: Error while fetching server API version: UnixHTTPConnectionPool(host='localhost', port=None): Read timed out. (read timeout=60)
I started by updating Docker — current version is v3.0.2.
Docker won't run on Big Sur

it says "Hi, this is a breaking change that can be fixed by upgrading your docker version to 2.3.0.4 Docker already pushed an update and upgrading docker fixes the bug of it not lunching on your Apple Big Sur." But I'd already updated to v3.0.2 and was still hitting
Error while fetching server API version
— including the issue where just typing
Later I noticed in the comments someone suggested "Go to Docker Troubleshoot -> Clean/purge data -> Reset to Factory defaults." That actually fixed it for me and Docker started running again.
I'm guessing the issue was that Docker didn't restart properly after the update, so when it tried to fetch the API it just timed out.
